entries.label: Create
entries.note: Growable array; most commonly used collection

entries.label: Add elements
entries.note: push appends; insert shifts elements right

entries.label: Access elements
entries.code:
let v = vec![1, 2, 3];
let third = v[2];          // panics if out of bounds
let third = v.get(2);      // returns Option<&T>
let first = v.first();     // Option<&T>
let last = v.last();       // Option<&T>
entries.note: Prefer .get() for safe access; indexing panics on out of bounds

entries.label: Remove elements
entries.code:
let mut v = vec![1, 2, 3, 4];
v.pop();           // removes last -> Some(4)
v.remove(0);       // removes at index, shifts left
v.retain(|&x| x > 1); // keep only elements > 1
v.clear();         // remove all
entries.note: pop is O(1); remove is O(n) due to shifting

entries.label: Iterate
entries.note: &v borrows; &mut v borrows mutably; v moves/consumes

entries.label: Slice operations
entries.note: Vec auto-derefs to &[T] for slice methods

entries.label: Sort and search
entries.note: sort requires Ord; sort_by for custom ordering

entries.label: Create
entries.note: Key must implement Eq + Hash

entries.label: Insert and update
entries.note: entry API for conditional insertion

entries.label: Access
entries.code:
let val = map.get("key");        // Option<&V>
let val = map["key"];             // panics if missing
let has = map.contains_key("key"); // bool
let len = map.len();
entries.note: Prefer .get() over indexing to avoid panics

entries.label: Update with entry
entries.note: Common pattern for counting occurrences

entries.label: Iterate
entries.note: Iteration order is not guaranteed

entries.label: Remove
entries.note: remove returns the removed value if it existed

entries.label: Create and use
entries.note: Sorted by key; keys must implement Ord

entries.label: Ordered iteration
entries.note: Always iterates in key order; unlike HashMap

entries.label: Range queries
entries.note: Efficient range queries; not available on HashMap

entries.label: First and last
entries.code:
let first = map.first_key_value(); // Some((&1, &"one"))
let last = map.last_key_value();   // Some((&3, &"three"))
entries.note: Quick access to min and max keys

entries.label: Create
entries.note: Unique elements; T must implement Eq + Hash

entries.label: Check membership
entries.code:
set.contains(&1); // true
set.len();        // number of elements
set.is_empty();   // bool
entries.note: O(1) average lookup time

entries.label: Set operations
entries.note: Mathematical set operations; returns iterators

entries.label: Subset/superset
entries.note: Check set relationships

entries.note: Double-ended queue; efficient push/pop at both ends

entries.label: Push and pop
entries.note: O(1) for push/pop at both ends

entries.label: Access
entries.note: front/back for ends; get for arbitrary index

entries.label: Use as queue
entries.note: FIFO: push_back + pop_front

entries.label: Create and use
entries.note: Sorted set; T must implement Ord

entries.label: Ordered iteration
entries.note: Always iterates in sorted order

entries.label: Range queries
entries.note: Efficient range iteration; min/max access

entries.label: Create and use
entries.note: Doubly-linked list; rarely needed in Rust (Vec/VecDeque usually better)

entries.label: Append lists
entries.note: O(1) append by moving pointers

entries.label: Collect into collection
entries.note: collect() can create any FromIterator collection

entries.label: Drain
entries.note: Remove a range and get the removed elements

entries.label: From iterator of tuples
entries.code:
let map: HashMap<String, i32> = vec!["a", "b", "c"]
    .into_iter()
    .enumerate()
    .map(|(i, s)| (s.to_string(), i as i32))
    .collect();
entries.note: HashMap can be collected from (K, V) iterator

entries.label: Capacity management
entries.note: Pre-allocate to avoid reallocations
